text: Chapters 2–9 walked through the supervised learning pipeline. We defined models that mapped observed data x to output values y and introduced loss functions that measured the quality of that mapping for a training dataset $\lbrace x_{i}, y_{i} \rbrace$ . Then

The defining characteristic of unsupervised learning models is that they are learned from a set of observed data $\lbrace x_{i} \rbrace$ in the absence of labels. All unsupervised models share this property, but they have diverse goals. They may be used to gen

This chapter introduces a taxonomy of unsupervised learning models and then discusses the desirable properties of models and how to measure their performance. The four subsequent chapters discuss four particular models: generative adversarial networks (GANs),

A common strategy in unsupervised learning is to define a mapping between the data examples x and a set of unseen latent variables z. These latent variables capture underlying structure in the dataset and usually have a lower dimension than the original data;
